title: T-093 — Seed: grant technical.* permissions to org-admin role family (technical-permission-seed.sql)
prompt:
# T-093 — Seed: grant technical.* permissions to the org-admin role family

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §3 (RBAC)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add an idempotent SQL seed migration `packages/db/migrations/<NNN>-technical-permission-seed.sql` (next free number; current highest is 151-outbox-event-type-enum-sot.sql, so use 152+) that GRANTS the technical.* permission family (added to the enum by T-091) to the org-admin role family, in BOTH the normalized `role_permissions` table AND the legacy `roles.permissions` jsonb cache, for every existing org, with an AFTER INSERT trigger so new orgs inherit it. This closes the X-1 unreachable-feature class: enum strings alone do NOT grant access — without this seed every technical page 403s for the org admin at Gate-5 (the exact live-deploy failure 01-npd hit; same class as migrations 116/146/148/149).

## Why this is required (X-1 standard)
Migration 080's NPD/role seed grants permissions to role CODES like npd_manager/core_user/admin, but the deployed org administrator is on the canonical org-admin role family ('org.access.admin' / 'org.platform.admin' / 'owner' / 'admin' / 'org_admin'), which receives NONE of the technical.* strings. Models EXACTLY on `packages/db/migrations/149-npd-permissions-org-admin-seed.sql`.

## Permission strings to grant (the technical.* family from T-091, count=11)
- technical.items.create
- technical.items.edit
- technical.items.deactivate
- technical.bom.create
- technical.bom.approve
- technical.bom.version_publish
- technical.bom.generate_batch
- technical.allergens.edit
- technical.cost.edit
- technical.d365.sync_trigger
- technical.product_spec.approve  (the pre-existing 11th technical string — include so the org admin can approve product specs)

## Implementation contract
- Create `public.seed_technical_permissions_for_org(p_org_id uuid)` SECURITY DEFINER, `set search_path = pg_catalog`, granting the v_perms array above to roles where `r.code = any(v_admin_roles) or r.slug = any(v_admin_roles)` with `v_admin_roles := array['org.access.admin','org.platform.admin','owner','admin','org_admin']`.
  - Normalized: `insert into public.role_permissions (role_id, permission) select ... on conflict (role_id, permission) do nothing`.
  - Legacy jsonb cache: `update public.roles set permissions = jsonb_agg(distinct ...)` union of existing + v_perms (set-deduplicated, sorted).
- `revoke all on function ... from public; revoke all on function ... from app_user;` for both functions.
- Create the on-insert trigger function + `drop trigger if exists trg_zzz_seed_technical_permissions on public.organizations; create trigger trg_zzz_seed_technical_permissions after insert on public.organizations for each row execute function ...` (zzz prefix so it fires after the 080 role-creating trigger).
- Backfill loop over every existing org.
- org_id Wave0 lock; RLS via app.current_org_id() (functions are SECURITY DEFINER, not app_user-callable).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/<NNN>-technical-permission-seed.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/technical-permission-seed.test.ts (or the repo's db-test location) [create] — integration test against real/local Postgres (pnpm db:up && pnpm db:test).

## Out of scope
- Adding the enum strings (T-091 owns the enum).
- Granting technical.* to non-admin functional roles (operator/technician matrix) — this seed is the org-admin reachability fix; finer role matrices are a follow-up if PRD §3 specifies them.
- Any factory_spec / BOM / cost table change.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs, when re-run, then it is idempotent (no-op; ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING + jsonb set-dedup).
2. Given an org admin (role 'org.access.admin' or 'admin'), when permissions resolve after the seed, then all 11 technical.* strings are granted in BOTH role_permissions and roles.permissions jsonb (integration test, real Postgres).
3. Given a newly inserted org, when the AFTER INSERT trigger fires, then the org-admin role inherits the technical.* family (no manual backfill needed).
4. Given a non-admin role, when permissions resolve, then it does NOT receive technical.* from this seed.
5. `pnpm db:up && pnpm db:test` green; RLS unaffected.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Write the integration test asserting the org-admin role resolves technical.bom.approve = allowed and a non-admin role = denied; run RED (seed not yet applied) — must fail.
2. Add the migration; re-run — must pass.
3. Run `pnpm db:up && pnpm db:test`; capture actual output.

## Risk red lines
- org_id Wave0 lock; app.current_org_id() scoping; functions SECURITY DEFINER + revoked from app_user/public.
- No hardcoded role UUIDs — match by role code/slug.
- Do not grant technical.* to the service/system role or to functional non-admin roles in this seed.
- Idempotent only — never DELETE/REVOKE existing grants.
- Enum strings must already exist (T-091) — this task does not edit the enum.

pipeline_inputs.description: Idempotent SQL seed migration granting the technical.* permission family to the org-admin role family (X-1 reachability fix). Models on migration 149-npd-permissions-org-admin-seed.sql. Without it every technical page 403s for the org admin at Gate-5.

Grant the 11 technical.* strings (10 from T-091 + the pre-existing technical.product_spec.approve). Highest migration today is 151; use the next free number (152+).

Granting technical.* to non-admin functional roles (operator/technician matrix).
Any factory_spec / BOM / cost table change.
